title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends
There has been much discussion about the hazards of a possible release of ammonia on board during a combustion process or during handling or storage. Read More

China reconfirmed this week what the dry bulk and container sectors were feeling during the first couple of trading months
Releasing the first major economic indicator since fully relaxing its zero-Covid policy, China reconfirmed this week what the dry bulk and container sectors were feeling during the first couple of trading months. Read More

Time for a breather to digest recent gains
It was only a month ago that market participants were faced with almost zero spot Capesize rates and dark clouds were gathering over the dry bulk market outlook.

children.title: The US experienced the second biggest bank failure in its history on Friday
section_path: The US experienced the second biggest bank failure in its history on Friday
children.section_path_text: The US experienced the second biggest bank failure in its history on Friday
children.summary: The US experienced the second biggest bank failure in its history on Friday when $209bn worth Silicon Valley Bank failed and was taken over by the regulator. However, despite the eerie echoes of 2008, we don't believe we are in a Lehman moment.

children.title: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
section_path: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.section_path_text: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.summary: The second week of March fueled optimism for a month of higher rates, as demand for Capesize vessels surprisingly increased in the face of increased Chinese demand for iron ore. Looking at the trend in demand growth (ton-days) from Australia to China (see chart above), we can see an upturn not seen in a similar period in the previous two years.

children.summary: In light of the collapse of the Silicon Valley Bank, the latest release of US inflation data did not make the Federal Reserve's job any easier.

children.title: VLCC rally to end?
children.summary: This week, we look at how sustainable the recent rally in VLCC rates towards China is, and examine the drivers behind declining Pacific MR freight rates.
